data.description:
## Context / Problem

Follow-up from fd71 (clayish-basaltine-bug), which wired the review bot to read `_usage` and emit token counts to CloudWatch — but only for the usage surfaced on the review's AGENT steps (Pass-2 `verify` + Pass-3 `decide`). The Pass-1 finder BATCH, often the largest token consumer, does not surface per-finder `_usage` on its batch step output: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` emits `findings`/`criteria_count` (no `_usage`), and the shared `run_pass1` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py`) returns only `findings`. So the CloudWatch token totals currently UNDERCOUNT by the finder pass.

## Approach

Aggregate each finder call's `_usage` inside `run_pass1` (each finder goes through `runner.run`, which attaches `_usage`) and thread a summed token dict up so the batch runner can place it on the `BatchRunResult.outputs` (e.g. `outputs['_usage']`). Then `finalize._attach_code_review_metrics` (and the plan-review metrics assembler) already sum `_usage` across steps, so finder tokens flow into `coverage['metrics']` automatically. This is a SHARED path (plan-review uses `run_pass1` too), so preserve the existing return contract carefully (add an out-param or extend the return tuple; behavior-preserving) and cover both gates.

## Scope

- Token counts only; no dollar cost.
- Must not change finder behavior, thresholds, or the plan-review path's outputs beyond adding the aggregated `_usage`.

## Testing

- Unit test that `run_pass1` returns/threads summed finder `_usage` and that the batch step output carries it.
- Unit test that `finalize` then includes finder tokens in `coverage['metrics']`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pass-1 finder per-call `_usage` is aggregated and appears on the batch step output; verified by a unit test.
- [ ] `coverage['metrics']` token totals include finder usage after the change; verified by a finalize unit test.
- [ ] Both code-review and plan-review paths remain green; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` pass; lands via Gerrit with Verified +1 and LLM-Review +1.
data.title: Surface Pass-1 finder token usage on the batch step output (complete review-bot token totals)
